zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Frances Brudenell於1442年6月20日出生在英國巴金哈姆爾時，她的父親Edmund Brudenell22歲，母親Philippa Englefield17歲。她於1474年與Sir John Iwardby在英國巴金哈姆爾華頓結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。她去世於1531年1月30日在英國巴金哈姆爾華頓享年88歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利 瓦爾。

nameMeanings.name: Brudenell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: ethnic name from Old Norman French Bretonnel, a diminutive of Bret ‘Breton’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Frances
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of   Francis .  In the 16th century the two spellings were used indiscriminately for both sexes, the distinction in spelling not being established until the 17th century.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Frances Brudenell was born on 20 June 1442, in Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Edmund Brudenell, was 22 and her mother, Philippa Englefield, was 17. She married Sir John Iwardby in 1474, in Quainton, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. She died on 30 January 1531, in Quainton, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 88, and was buried in Aylesbury Vale, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Frances Brudenell est née le 20 juin 1442, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Brudenell, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Philippa Englefield, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Iwardby en 1474, à Quainton, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 30 janvier 1531, à Quainton, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 88 ans, et a été enterrée à Aylesbury Vale, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Frances Brudenellが1442年6月20日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父Edmund Brudenellは22歳，母Philippa Englefieldは17歳であった。1474年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアクアイントンでSir John Iwardbyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1531年1月30日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアクアイントンで88歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアエイレスブリー ベルにて埋葬された。

ko.Amersham with Coleshill, Buckinghamshire: 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 어머샤 위스 콜레시
ko.lifeSummary: Joan Frances Brudenell(이)가 1442년 6월 20일 영국 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Brudenell(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Philippa Englefield(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1474년 영국 벅잉함실르 퀄인턴에서 Sir John Iwardby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1531년 1월 30일 영국 벅잉함실르 퀄인턴에서 88세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 아일레스버이 발에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir John Iwardby em 1474, em Quainton, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhas. ru.Amersham with Coleshill, Buckinghamshire: амиршам вит колешилл, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Frances Brudenell родилась 20 июня 1442 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Brudenell было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Philippa Englefield, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Iwardby в 1474 г. в местности куеинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Она умерла 30 января 1531 г. в местности куеинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 88 лет и была похоронена в местности аилесбури веил, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Sir John Iwardby en 1474, en Quainton, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijas. Murió el 30 de enero de 1531, en Quainton, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 88 años, y fue sepultada en Aylesbury Vale, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
